sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_dates (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, event_date TIMESTAMP, days_offset INTEGER);
INSERT INTO test_dates (id, event_date, days_offset) VALUES (1, TIMESTAMP '1992-03-22', 5), (2, TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01', NULL), (3, TIMESTAMP NULL, 10);
SELECT id, dayname(event_date + days_offset) AS weekday_name FROM test_dates WHERE event_date IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, dayname((SELECT MIN(event_date) FROM test_dates WHERE days_offset IS NOT NULL) + 3) AS min_weekday_name FROM test_dates;

WITH date_summary AS (SELECT event_date, COUNT(*) AS total_events FROM test_dates GROUP BY event_date) SELECT dayname(MIN(event_date) + (SELECT total_events FROM date_summary ORDER BY total_events DESC LIMIT 1)) AS most_common_weekday FROM date_summary;
SELECT DISTINCT dayname(TIMESTAMP '1992-03-22' + (SELECT offset_value FROM test_offset WHERE offset_value IS NOT NULL LIMIT 1)) AS test_dayname FROM test_dates;
SELECT event_date, dayname(event_date + CASE WHEN days_offset IS NULL THEN 0 ELSE days_offset END) AS resultant_weekday FROM test_dates WHERE days_offset IS NOT NULL;
SELECT event_date, dayname(event_date + (SELECT MIN(days_offset) FROM test_dates WHERE days_offset IS NOT NULL)) AS day_name FROM test_dates GROUP BY event_date HAVING day_name IS NOT NULL;
